name: Almonte and El Rocío Doñana Loop
name_original: El Rocío a Almonte – circular por el Parque Natural de Doñana
description: Explore the Parque Natural de Doñana on this 32.5-mile moderate mountain bike loop from El Rocío, featuring marshes and pine forests.
region_intro: Mountain biking in Parc National De Doñana offers routes through a diverse landscape shaped by the Atlantic Ocean and the Guadalquivir River. The terrain features extensive marshes, shifting sand dunes, and pristine beaches, alongside pine forests and Mediterranean scrubland. Elevation changes are generally modest, with most routes traversing flat tracks or gentle slopes. This UNESCO World Heritage Site provides an eco-friendly way to experience its unique biodiversity. Best mountain bike trails in Parc National De Doñana The most popular mountain bike trail is Almonte and El Rocío Doñana Loop, a 32.5 miles (52.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 1 minute to complete. This route traverses pine forests and marshes, offering views of the park's natural features. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Matalascañas Dune – Mazagón Beach Cliffs loop from Sector K - Junco, a difficult 43.3 miles (69.7 km) path. This route explores coastal paths, sand dunes, and beach cliffs, requiring good fitness. Local mountain bikers also love the Doñana Beach – Castilla Beach loop from Chiringuito El Pichi, a 34.8 miles (56.0 km) trail leading through coastal areas and beaches, often completed in about 4 hours 26 minutes. Mountain biking in Parc National De Doñana is defined by sand dunes, extensive marshes, and pine forests, with options for different ability levels. The routes in Parc National De Doñana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Parc National De Doñana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there any specific regulations for mountain biking in Parc National De Doñana?
A: Yes, as a UNESCO World Heritage Site and protected area, access to certain zones within Parc National De Doñana is restricted. While mountain biking is generally allowed on designated trails, some highly protected areas may require guided tours or specific permits. It's always best to check with the park authorities or visitor centers for the most current regulations before planning your ride.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Parc National De Doñana?
A: Parc National De Doñana offers a wide selection of routes, with over 75 mountain bike trails available. These range from easy to difficult, ensuring options for various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Parc National De Doñana?
A: The terrain in Parc National De Doñana is diverse, shaped by the Atlantic Ocean and the Guadalquivir River. You can expect to ride through extensive marshes, shifting sand dunes, and pristine beaches, as well as pine forests and Mediterranean scrubland. While some routes feature flat tracks, others include gentle slopes, with overall elevation changes being modest.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Parc National De Doñana?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Parc National De Doñana is typically during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May). Summers can be very hot, making longer rides less comfortable. Spring and autumn also offer excellent opportunities for birdwatching as migratory birds pass through the marshes.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Parc National De Doñana?
A: Yes, Parc National De Doñana offers a good selection of trails for beginners. There are 21 easy routes, providing a gentle introduction to the park's landscapes. These trails often traverse flat tracks through pine forests and offer an enjoyable experience without significant technical challenges.

Q: Can I find challenging mountain bike routes in Parc National De Doñana?
A: Yes, for experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, there are 5 difficult routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31519464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matalascañas Dune – Mazagón Beach Cliffs loop from Sector K - Junco&lt;/a&gt;, a 69.7 km path that explores coastal areas, sand dunes, and beach cliffs, requiring very good fitness.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available in Parc National De Doñana?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Parc National De Doñana are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995547137/el-rocio-a-almonte-circular-por-el-parque-natural-de-donana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rocío a Almonte – circular por el Parque Natural de Doñana&lt;/a&gt;, which is 52.3 km long and traverses pine forests and marshes.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Along the mountain bike trails, you can encounter various natural and cultural landmarks. The park features extensive marshes, dynamic sand dunes like the Asperillo Dunes, and pristine beaches such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4557220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castilla Beach&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass by the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/885349" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of El Rocío&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4091378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco de la Boca Trail&lt;/a&gt; is another notable point of interest.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while mountain biking in Doñana?
A: Doñana is a critical refuge for diverse wildlife. While mountain biking, you might spot various bird species, especially in the marsh areas, including flamingos, gulls, and storks. The park is also home to mammals like fallow deer, Spanish red deer, and wild boars. Keep an eye out for the endangered Iberian lynx and Spanish Imperial Eagle, though sightings of these rare species are less common.

Q: Is Parc National De Doñana suitable for family mountain biking trips?
A: Yes, Parc National De Doñana can be suitable for family mountain biking, especially on its easier and moderate trails. The relatively flat terrain and scenic routes through pine forests and along marshes offer a pleasant experience for families. Some services in the region even offer children's bikes, making it easier for younger riders to participate.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking in Parc National De Doñana?
A: Parking is generally available near the main visitor centers and popular starting points for trails. For instance, routes originating from El Rocío or Matalascañas typically have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check specific route details or visitor center information for precise parking locations.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Parc National De Doñana?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 200 reviews. Reviewers often praise the unique biodiversity, the tranquil routes through pine forests and marshes, and the opportunity to experience the park's natural beauty in an eco-friendly way. The varied terrain, from coastal paths to wetlands, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in Parc National De Doñana?
A: Yes, Parc National De Doñana offers several long-distance routes for those looking for extended rides. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40005987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanlúcar de Barrameda Beach – Doñana Beach loop from Matalascañas&lt;/a&gt; is an 88.1 km trail that takes approximately 6 hours and 30 minutes to complete, leading through coastal areas and beaches.
tips: The Rocina stream is one of the main sources of water to the marshes of the Doñana National Park. In its last section it becomes the mother channel of the Rocio marshes, leaving behind an unbeatable landscape for bird watching. At the end of La Rocina is the Charco de la Boca, which maintains water throughout the year and is essential for the breeding and feeding of numerous aquatic species.

This is a truly unique town, just leave your bike on the outside with all that loose sand. Plenty to see, beautiful buildings, green oases, significance for pilgrims, many restaurants and lots of loose sand.

The Doñana Park nature reserve is home to a wide variety of ecosystems and a wealth of flora and fauna. It is also a very important migratory stage for thousands of birds en route to the south or north. Sometimes the trails that go into these pine forests are quite sandy.

Almonte is one of the most prominent towns in Huelva. The peculiar village of El Rocío, where the renowned Marian pilgrimage is celebrated, and the spectacular Matalascañas beach, completely surrounded by the Doñana National Park, are the best-known spots.

faqs.question: How difficult is the El Rocío a Almonte mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as a moderate mountain bike ride. It requires good fitness, and advanced riding skills are considered necessary, especially given some sandy sections within the pine forests.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers varied terrain over its 52.3 kilometers. You'll encounter extensive marshes and pine forests. While mostly flat or gently sloped, some sections within the pine forests can be quite sandy, adding to the challenge. The path includes 45.5 km of designated path, 3.67 km of road, and segments of street, singletrack, state road, and access road. Approximately 27.7 km are unpaved, 16.6 km are natural, with other sections being asphalt, paved, and gravel.

faqs.question: What points of interest will I see along the El Rocío a Almonte route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience both natural beauty and cultural highlights. The trail passes by the Rocina stream and the Charco de la Boca (La Rocina), which is excellent for birdwatching. You'll also ride through the Pine Forest of Doñana National Park. Culturally, the route connects to the distinctive El Rocío Village, known for its unique architecture, and Almonte, with its white houses and historical buildings like the Plaza Virgen del Rocío and Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción, Almonte.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Doñana National Park, avoiding the extreme heat of summer and potential heavy rains in winter. These seasons also provide excellent opportunities for birdwatching.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Regulations for dogs in Doñana National Park can be strict to protect wildlife. It's advisable to check the current rules for pets within the park before planning your ride, as they may be restricted or require leashes in designated areas.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Parque Natural de Doñana?
faqs.answer: While the Parque Natural de Doñana is a protected area, generally, there are no specific entrance fees or permits required for cycling on designated public trails like this one. However, it's always recommended to check the official park website for any updated regulations or specific requirements for certain activities or zones before your visit.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead for this circular route?
faqs.answer: Given that the route is a circular trail starting and ending in El Rocío, you can typically find parking within or near El Rocío Village. Look for public parking areas, especially near the main access points to the park trails from the village.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: As the trail passes through the UNESCO World Heritage Site of Doñana National Park, it offers excellent opportunities for wildlife spotting, particularly birdwatching. The marshes and the Rocina stream are crucial habitats for a wide array of aquatic species and birds. Keep an eye out for various bird species, and if you're lucky, you might spot some of the park's other iconic animals, though many are elusive.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will find yourself on parts of the Transandalus, Sendero del Charco de la Boca, Camino del Algarrobo, Camino de la Cerca, Raya Real, and Paseo Marismeño, among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, both El Rocío Village and Almonte offer various amenities. In El Rocío, you'll find restaurants, bars, and accommodation options, especially catering to pilgrims and visitors. Almonte also provides a range of services, including eateries and places to stay.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: For this moderate 52.3 km mountain bike ride, you should bring plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), a basic bike repair kit, and a first-aid kit. Given the varied terrain, including sandy sections, appropriate mountain biking gear and footwear are essential. A fully charged phone and a navigation device (like komoot) are also highly recommended.

waypoints.name: Pine Forest of Doñana National Park
Plaza Virgen del Rocío and Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción, Almonte
